summary: Tested Redis caching and pagination for Vendors and Income/Expenses pages. Backend APIs return correct paginated response format. Fixed pagination stability bug where entries with the same date could appear on multiple pages. UI shows pagination controls and 'Showing X of Y entries' text correctly.

issues: React console warning: 'Encountered two children with the same key' - non-critical but should use unique key (entry_id) for list rendering

passed_tests: Backend: /api/vendors returns paginated response with items, total, page, page_size, total_pages
Backend: /api/vendors search parameter works with pagination
Backend: /api/income-expenses returns paginated response with items, total, page, page_size, total_pages
Backend: /api/income-expenses page 2 returns different data than page 1 (after fix)
Backend: Redis caching working for vendors API (cache key vendors:list:*)
Backend: Redis caching working for income-expenses API (cache key ie:list:*)
Backend: Items count does not exceed page_size for vendors
Backend: Items count does not exceed page_size for income-expenses
Frontend: Income & Expenses page shows 'Showing 10 of 40 entries' text
Frontend: Income & Expenses page shows pagination numbers (1, 2, 3, 4)
Frontend: Income & Expenses page Previous/Next buttons visible and functional
Frontend: Clicking page number navigates to different page
Frontend: Vendors page shows search bar
Frontend: Vendors page shows 'Showing X of Y exchangers' text
action_items: Consider adding unique keys to React list rendering in IncomeExpenses.js to fix console warnings (use entry_id instead of index)

success_rate.backend: 100% (10/10 passed, 1 skipped - vendors page 2 test skipped due to only 1 page of vendors)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Pagination and Redis caching implemented and tested. Backend APIs /api/vendors and /api/income-expenses return paginated responses with items, total, page, page_size, total_pages. Frontend displays pagination controls with page numbers and Previous/Next buttons. Fixed a pagination stability bug by adding secondary sort by entry_id. Redis is running on localhost:6379 and cache keys are being created (ie:list:*, vendors:list:*). There's a React console warning about duplicate keys that is non-critical.

bug_fixed.description: Pagination overlap bug - same entry appearing on both page 1 and page 2
bug_fixed.root_cause: Sorting by date alone without secondary sort key caused instability for records with same date
bug_fixed.fix: Added secondary sort by entry_id to ensure stable pagination
